Pneumocystis jirovecii is characterized as being dimorphic, meaning it exhibits different morphologies under varying environmental conditions. It can switch between a unicellular form (trophozoites) and multicellular structures (cysts). Dimorphism refers to having two distinct forms – such as yeast phase vs mycelial growth pattern observed in Histoplasma, Blastomyces, etc., hence all these are considered dimorphic fungi except D: **Pneumocystis jirovecii**.
